msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 126 is again an attachment-only continuation. I used only the attached mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md; I did not substitute any File Library material.

Turn 126 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state is unchanged from Turn 125:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether the two profinite one-relator quotients are isomorphic over the fixed tame quotient G, equivalently whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH satisfying πH∘Φ=πS.The attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement:∃Φ:F∼

F over Gsuch thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this gives Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,and therefore descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,that is,ΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G isomorphism condition. The history repeatedly freezes this as the theorem-level state and says no further Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, A2 -subcase, t-layer, or exceptional finite case should be reopened under attachment-only inputs.

The frozen proof chain remains the uniform certificate chain:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+Certificates D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate is also unchanged. The current attachments are only the prompt/problem/history set. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. Ledger-recorded verifier claims support the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict Certificate D gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 126 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 125. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN126-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
